strategic_profile.executive_summary: San Diego State University (SDSU) is a highly selective, R1 public research university located in Southern California. As the flagship-equivalent of the CSU system, it offers a vibrant, sports-oriented campus culture paired with rigorous, impacted academic programs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Out-of-state applicants are accepted at an 87% rate compared to 31% for in-state residents.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Geographic arbitrage is a massive factor; non-residents have a significant statistical advantage due to institutional financial incentives.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 100% of majors and pre-majors are classified as 'impacted'.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Admission to the university does not guarantee admission to your desired degree; students must clear strict internal GPA hurdles to graduate.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Ranked #22 for Top Party Schools with heavy Greek and D1 sports participation.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The environment is highly social and extroverted, suited for students seeking a traditional, spirited college experience.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant, highly social campus heavily influenced by Division I athletics (Snapdragon Stadium), a massive Greek system, and proximity to beaches and downtown.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Highly accessible; an on-campus MTS trolley station allows for car-free transit to downtown San Diego, beaches, and the international border.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Recently designated as an R1 institution (Very High Research Activity) by the Carnegie Classification, indicating massive growth in federal funding and undergraduate research opportunities.

current_status.test_policy_details: Strictly Test-Blind. SAT/ACT scores are not considered for admissions, only for post-enrollment course placement.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist activity rebounded massively in 2024 compared to previous zero-admit years.
No students were admitted from the waitlist in 2022.

gpa.notes: CSU calculates GPA based only on A-G coursework taken in 10th and 11th grades.

colleges.name: University-Wide
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students applying should be prepared to perform well in prerequisite courses during their freshman and sophomore years, as access to upper-division courses requires clearing impaction.
colleges.housing_profile: First-year students from outside the local area are required to live on campus for two years.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, socially active students who can balance rigorous impaction hurdles with a heavy distraction environment.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.notes: The most popular major on campus.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Must earn a B or better in high school intermediate algebra, biology, and chemistry with labs.
majors.notes: Highly restricted and arguably the most competitive major at the university.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Preference given to applicants with strong grades in high school science and math.

supplemental_requirements.details: Applicants must successfully pass an audition or portfolio review.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Local San Diego county residents receive massive priority in the admissions formula.

major_selection_tactics: Select an alternate major on the application, as SDSU will occasionally admit to the alternate if the primary choice is full.
Ensure A-G requirements are not just met, but heavily exceeded with honors/AP courses.
college_ranking_tactics: If utilizing the community college transfer route, attend a San Diego area community college (e.g., Mesa College) to receive local priority.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: List a slightly less impacted major as an alternate to secure general university admission, though internal transfer later can be difficult.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate for in-state; per-unit surcharge for out-of-state.

scholarships.name: SDSU Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Financial stipend and prestigious scholar designation.

scholarships.name: SDSU Advantage Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Targeted at highly qualified out-of-state and international students.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 3 or higher grant college credit.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to bypass general education requirements (e.g., AP English waives RWS 100/200).

ib_policy.general_rule: Scores of 4 to 7 on Higher Level exams grant credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum of 70 units from community college transfer coursework will apply toward the bachelor's degree unit total.

what_it_takes: A weighted CSU GPA approaching 4.0 or higher.
Graduating from a local San Diego high school is the biggest advantage.
Exceptional grades in math and science for STEM applicants.
essay_tips: Do not write one; the CSU application does not accept essays or personal statements.
red_flags: Applying to Nursing or Business as an out-of-state student with a low GPA.
Having any D or F grades in A-G coursework.
insights: SDSU's shift to test-blind means your transcript is your entire application.
Impaction is real; do not assume you can easily switch into Computer Science or Nursing once enrolled.
